sections.paragraph:
αἰσχύνη, ης, ἡ [αἶσχος ‘shame/disgrace’]

—1. ‘experience of humiliation or disapproval’, shame, disgrace, ignominy Lk 14:9; Hb 12:2; Rv 3:18. By metonymy of abstract for concrete

—2.‘repulsive deed’ 2 Cor 4:2; Phil 3:19. In imagery of dirty foam cast up on a shore shameful deed Jd 13.—The positive aspect of ‘modesty’ or ‘reverence’ does not appear in the NT.
